tool.name: silent
tool.summary: Report synonymous single-codon edits that create exact forward-strand restriction sites

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `silent` reports conservative synonymous single-codon edits that create an exact canonical DNA restriction site in a coding sequence while preserving translation. EMBOSS-RS v1 treats this as a bounded analytical design aid rather than an enzyme-database or sequence-optimization system.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local coding-DNA input path plus one canonical DNA site string. Inputs must validate as strict coding sequences under the standard genetic code, with canonical DNA codons and no internal stops. RNA residues, ambiguous nucleotides, and non-coding inputs are rejected.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits a stable table report with columns `record`, `site`, `site_start`, `site_end`, `codon_index`, `codon_start`, `codon_end`, `amino_acid`, `original_codon`, `replacement_codon`, and `mutated_sequence`.

sections.title: Model
sections.content: This first release considers only exact forward-strand site matches and only single-codon synonymous substitutions. A candidate is reported only when the edit creates one new exact site occurrence that was absent from the original sequence.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs silent`. Validation currently covers deterministic synonymous creation candidates against a committed coding-DNA fixture and non-coding-input rejection.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not model enzyme databases, reverse-complement matching, multi-edit optimization, codon-usage optimization, or feature-coordinate remapping. It is intentionally limited to canonical DNA site strings of length at least four.

artifacts.label: Restriction-site creation coding fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed coding-DNA fixture that is one synonymous codon edit away from an exact EcoRI site.

examples.title: Report a synonymous edit that creates an EcoRI site
examples.description: Loads a committed coding-DNA fixture and reports deterministic single-codon synonymous edits that create the exact site `GAATTC`.

expected_outputs.label: Restriction-site creation candidate table
expected_outputs.description: A stable tabular report containing one row per synonymous single-codon edit that creates the exact target site.
